questions.question: Under the Revised Penal Code of the Philippines, the principle that penal laws shall be obligatory upon all persons who live or sojourn in the Philippines is known as:
questions.explanation: The principle of generality (Article 14, Civil Code; Article 2, RPC) states that penal laws are binding upon all persons who live or sojourn in Philippine territory — citizens and foreigners alike — with limited exceptions (diplomats, heads of state).

questions.question: The Latin term 'actus non facit reum nisi mens sit rea' means:

choices.A: An act done in self-defense is not a crime
choices.B: An act does not make a person guilty unless the mind is also guilty
choices.C: Ignorance of the law is no excuse
choices.D: Every crime requires a physical act

questions.explanation: This Latin maxim, foundational to criminal law, means 'An act does not make a person guilty unless the mind is also guilty.' It establishes the requirement of criminal intent (mens rea) alongside the criminal act (actus reus) for most offenses.

questions.question: Under the Revised Penal Code, crimes are classified according to their gravity as:

choices.A: Serious, Less Serious, and Light Offenses
choices.B: Grave Felonies, Less Grave Felonies, and Light Felonies
choices.C: Capital Offenses, Major Crimes, and Minor Crimes

questions.explanation: Under Article 9 of the Revised Penal Code, felonies are classified as: Grave (punishable by capital punishment or afflictive penalties), Less Grave (punishable by correctional penalties), and Light (punishable by light penalties).

questions.question: Which of the following is a justifying circumstance under the Revised Penal Code?

choices.B: Acting in defense of a stranger with unlawful aggression and reasonable means

questions.explanation: Article 11 of the RPC lists justifying circumstances: self-defense, defense of relatives, defense of strangers, avoidance of evil, fulfillment of duty, and obedience to orders. Defense of strangers requires: (1) unlawful aggression, (2) reasonable means of repelling it, and (3) person defending was not induced by revenge or resentment.

questions.question: A crime is in the FRUSTRATED stage when:

choices.A: The offender commences the commission of a felony by overt acts but does not complete it
choices.B: The offender performs all acts of execution, but the crime is not produced due to causes independent of his will
choices.C: All acts are executed and the crime is produced
choices.D: The offender plans the crime but takes no action

questions.explanation: Article 6 of the RPC: Frustrated stage — the offender performs ALL acts of execution that would produce the felony, but the felony is NOT produced due to causes INDEPENDENT of the perpetrator's will. Compare: Attempted (not all acts done), Consummated (crime is produced).

questions.question: The legal principle 'When there is conspiracy, the act of one is the act of all' means:

choices.A: All participants must personally commit every act
choices.B: All co-conspirators are equally liable for the crime committed in furtherance of the conspiracy
choices.C: Only the principal offender is liable for the crime
choices.D: Conspirators are only liable for their individual acts

questions.explanation: Under conspiracy doctrine, once conspiracy is established, the act of any conspirator in furtherance of the conspiracy is the act of all. All conspirators are liable as principals regardless of which specific acts each one performed.

questions.question: Reclusion Perpetua under the Revised Penal Code carries a prison term of:

choices.D: Not less than 20 years and not more than 40 years

questions.explanation: Reclusion Perpetua (Article 27, RPC as amended by RA 7659) is imprisonment for 20 years and 1 day to 40 years. It is an indivisible penalty. Note: Unlike life imprisonment under special laws, reclusion perpetua allows accessory penalties.

questions.question: Republic Act 9165, the Comprehensive Dangerous Drugs Act of 2002, categorizes illegal drugs into how many schedules?
questions.explanation: RA 9165 categorizes dangerous drugs into two schedules (annexed to the law): Dangerous Drugs (Schedule I: e.g., opium, heroin, cocaine, marijuana, MDMA) and Controlled Precursors and Essential Chemicals (Schedule II).

questions.question: Under the Miranda doctrine, a person under custodial investigation must be informed of all of the following EXCEPT:

choices.C: The right to be released on bail
choices.D: That anything said can be used against them

questions.explanation: The Miranda rights (Article III, Sec. 12, 1987 Constitution; RA 7438) require informing suspects of: (1) right to remain silent; (2) anything said may be used against them; (3) right to counsel; (4) if indigent, counsel will be provided. The right to bail is a separate constitutional right, not a Miranda warning.

questions.question: A recidivist under the Revised Penal Code is one who, at the time of his trial, has been previously convicted by final judgment of another crime embraced in the same title of the RPC. Recidivism is:
questions.explanation: Under Article 14 of the RPC, recidivism is listed as an aggravating circumstance — it increases the penalty of the crime. It requires: (1) prior conviction by final judgment, (2) conviction of a crime under the same title of the RPC, and (3) being convicted again of the same or a related crime.
